activities_projects.title: Identifying Economic Sectors Around Us
activities_projects.objective: To identify primary, secondary, and tertiary sector activities in daily life.
steps: List 10 occupations found in your locality.
Classify each into primary, secondary, or tertiary sector.
Share and discuss answers in class.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the classification of economic activities into three sectors.
activities_projects.evaluation: Check correct classification and participation in discussion.

activities_projects.title: Sector Sorting Game
activities_projects.objective: To differentiate between the three sectors in a fun way.
steps: Write different occupations on paper slips.
Pick one slip at a time and identify the sector.
Explain the reason for classification.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students clearly understand differences between primary, secondary, and tertiary sectors.
activities_projects.evaluation: Assess correctness of answers and explanation.

activities_projects.title: Pie Chart of Workforce Distribution
steps: Use textbook data for workforce distribution.
Draw a pie chart showing percentage in each sector.
Label all sectors clearly.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn to interpret employment distribution among sectors.
activities_projects.evaluation: Check accuracy of pie chart and labeling.

activities_projects.title: Role Play of Economic Sectors
activities_projects.objective: To understand how sectors are interdependent.
steps: Assign roles such as farmer, factory worker, shopkeeper.
Each student explains their role in the economy.
Discuss how each sector depends on others.
activities_projects.evaluation: Evaluate clarity of explanation and participation.

activities_projects.title: Public vs Private Sector Table
activities_projects.objective: To differentiate between public and private sectors.
steps: List five examples of public sector industries.
List five examples of private sector industries.
Write two differences between them.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand ownership differences in economic sectors.
activities_projects.evaluation: Check correctness of examples and differences.

activities_projects.title: Service Sector Observation Activity
activities_projects.objective: To observe service sector activities in daily life.
steps: Observe five service providers in your area (bank, hospital, school).
Note the services provided.
Explain why they belong to the tertiary sector.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students identify features of the tertiary sector.
activities_projects.evaluation: Assess accuracy of identification and explanation.

activities_projects.title: Primary Sector Poster
activities_projects.objective: To understand the importance of the primary sector.
steps: Draw activities like farming, fishing, mining.
Write two lines on why the primary sector is important.
Display poster in class.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students recognize the importance of natural resource-based activities.
activities_projects.evaluation: Evaluate relevance of content and creativity.

activities_projects.title: Income Source Survey
activities_projects.objective: To identify main income sources in families.
steps: Ask five classmates about their parents' occupations.
Classify them into sectors.
Count which sector has more representation.
activities_projects.evaluation: Check classification accuracy and simple analysis.

activities_projects.title: GDP Contribution Chart
activities_projects.objective: To understand sector-wise contribution to GDP.
steps: Collect GDP percentage data from textbook.
Draw a bar graph showing contribution of each sector.
Write two observations.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand contribution of sectors to national income.
activities_projects.evaluation: Assess graph accuracy and observation quality.

activities_projects.title: Organized vs Unorganized Sector Activity
activities_projects.objective: To differentiate between organized and unorganized sectors.
steps: List five organized sector jobs and five unorganized sector jobs.
Write two differences between them.
Discuss benefits of organized sector employment.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand working conditions and job security differences.
activities_projects.evaluation: Check correctness of examples and clarity of differences.
